claims.claim: Pipecat send_with_retry reconnects then replays the in-flight failed message
claims.found: L122 async def send_with_retry; L134 success = await self._try_reconnect(...); L138 await self._websocket.send(message)  # retry same message

claims.claim: LiveKit preserves _start_time_offset across reconnect attempts so transcript timestamps stay linear
claims.found: L390 comment 'so that returned transcripts can have linear timestamps'; L396 self._start_time_offset += time.time() - last_start_time inside retry loop; offset init once at L337=0.0, only += , never reset

claims.claim: Deepgram quote: prior conversational context restored through injected state rather than restarting cold; when recovery fails communicate clearly
claims.found: L593-595: 'When reconnection is possible, prior conversational context should be restored through injected state rather than restarting cold. When recovery fails, the system should communicate clearly rather than leaving silence.'

claims.claim: Failure-mode appendix inspection target: whether state is properly restored after reconnect
claims.found: L2092: 'Connection timeout settings, reconnection backoff logic, whether state is [properly restored after reconnect]...'

claims.claim: Loss of Context appendix: prior turns retained and injected into reasoning, kept above socket
claims.found: L2105 'Loss of Context or Forgetting Mid-Conversation'; L2109 'How prior turns are retained and injected into reasoning.'

claims.claim: Reconnect re-runs the service's own _connect_websocket() which re-sends provider config (model/language/encoding/keyterms) before resuming audio
claims.found: WebsocketService._reconnect_websocket (L77-78) calls _disconnect_websocket() then _connect_websocket(); deepgram _connection_handler (L631) rebuilds connect_kwargs via _build_connect_kwargs() carrying model/language/encoding/keyterm on every (re)connect
send_with_retry present in pipecat base WebsocketService; LiveKit stt.py has no equivalent single-message replay (retry loop re-runs _run, not a buffered frame)

claims.claim: Conversation/LLM history lives in orchestrator context above socket, not re-sent over recovered socket
claims.found: Consistent with deepgram Loss-of-Context appendix (state persistence/memory layer separate from transport); no speech-layer clone persists LLM history on the socket. Reasonable architectural framing, not contradicted.

summary: All 8 checkable assertions hold. Both frontmatter code_refs (websocket_service.py:122 send_with_retry replay; stt.py:390 _start_time_offset linear timestamps) are accurate at the cited lines. All three deepgram-ebook line cites (594, 2092, 2105-2111) match the raw parsed PDF exactly. The _connect re-sends-config mechanism is confirmed in pipecat's deepgram service (_build_connect_kwargs rebuilt every reconnect). No WRONG or MISCITED claims.
